articles.headline: Bukayo Saka 'ready to go' for England World Cup opener amid Achilles worries
articles.description: Bukayo Saka has played down concerns over his fitness by insisting "I'm ready to go" on the eve of England's World Cup opener against Croatia.

images.name: Bukayo Saka [1296x729]
images.caption: Bukayo Saka has said he is "ready to go" for England's tournament opener against Croatia -- having played just 27 minutes against Costa Rica.
images.credit: Photo by Chris Arjoon/Icon Sportswire via Getty Images

images.name: Bukayo Saka says he is 'ready to go' ahead of England opener
images.caption: Bukayo Saka says he is 'ready to go' ahead of England opener

articles.headline: Spain tend to start slowly at World Cups, but nobody expected Cape Verde draw
articles.description: Spain have a history of starting slowly at World Cups before finding their form, but nobody -- not even them -- expected to open 2026 with a draw against tiny Cape Verde.

images.name: Spain woe v Cabo Verde 260615 [1296x729]
images.credit: Maddie Meyer - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images

images.name: Burley slams 'extremely pedestrian' Spain after Cape Verde draw
images.caption: Burley slams 'extremely pedestrian' Spain after Cape Verde draw

articles.headline: Spain won't panic despite draw with 'inferior' Cape Verde - Luis de la Fuente
articles.description: Coach Luis de la Fuente insisted that tournament favourites Spain won't panic after being held to a 0-0 draw by underdogs Cape Verde.

images.name: Spain [1296x729]
images.caption: Spain head coach Luis de la Fuente won't panic despite a draw with 'inferior' Cape Verde.
images.alt: Ferran Torres, Pedri, Fabian Ruiz and Mikel Oyarzabal of Spain react during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group H match between Spain and Cabo Verde.
images.credit: Photo by Maddie Meyer - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images.
